questions.question: There was a table in the room. There were no chairs in the room. (conjunction)

options.option: There was a table in the room but there were no chairs in the room.
There was a table in the room as there were no chairs in the room.
There was neither a table nor a chair in the room.
There was a table in the room and there were no chairs in the room.

questions.explanation: In this sentence 'but' is a conjunction which joins two sentences. The conjunction 'but' is used to connect ideas that contrast. Here 'but' also points out to contrast that there is a table but no chairs.

questions.question: Tina wants to come back from the hostel. She is homesick.

options.option: Tina wants to come back from the hostel yet she is homesick.
Tina wants to come back from the hostel but she is homesick.
Tina wants to come back from the hostel so she is homesick.
Tina wants to come back from the hostel because she is homesick.

questions.explanation: In this sentence 'because' is the conjunction which joins two sentences and gives the reason.

questions.question: Your friend is really intelligent.  Here, the word "Really" ________.
questions.explanation: An adverb is a word that modifies an adjective, verb, or another adverb expressing manner, place, time, or degree. Generally, adverbs of degree can describe adjectives or other adverbs. Here 'really' is an adverb that describes or modifies the adjective 'intelligent'.

questions.question: I have to go to school tomorrow so I have to get up early.
questions.explanation: An adverb is a word that modifies the meaning of an adjective, verb, or other adverb expressing manner, place, time, or degree. Here 'early' is the adverb of time that describes or qualifies the verb get up.
